authoritative_sources.title: Home Health Quality Reporting Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, and official program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This is the federal baseline for home-health reporting obligations and quality workflow expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: OASIS home health data requirements and reporting references
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, and official program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OASIS-related publication references define required assessment data and reporting mechanics for home-health reporting support.

authoritative_sources.title: Nursing Home Data and quality reporting guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS quality-reporting guidance defines what outcome data, incidents, and quality artifacts are relevant in SNF/NF contexts.

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 441.311 Reporting requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: Legal Information Institute, Cornell (Federal regulation mirror)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, or official policy source
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Directly defines what HCBS states must report and how reporting cycles are structured.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: submission cadence and reporting obligation review
used_for: escalation when requirements conflict with tenant context

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 441.302 Incident management and incident reporting
authoritative_sources.publisher: Legal Information Institute, Cornell (Federal regulation mirror)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, or official policy source
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines incident-management reporting obligations that materially change failure handling and escalation expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 483.20 Resident assessment obligations
authoritative_sources.publisher: Legal Information Institute, Cornell (Federal regulation mirror)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, or official policy source
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines assessment timing, reassessment cadence, and record encoding duties that shape reporting evidence requirements.

authoritative_sources.title: Health Home Quality Reporting
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Supports adjacent elder/chronic reporting obligations and cross-service disclosure expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: Health and Community-Based Services quality measure resources
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides quality-measure data requirements and reporting infrastructure context used to anchor evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: evidence normalization and output shape control

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Standard guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (Office for Civil Rights)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines minimum-necessary disclosure constraints and is directly relevant to stakeholder communication and data release decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: NAICS Sector 62 and related health/social assistance taxonomy
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Establishes the industry baseline and confirms elder-care social assistance positioning for non-generic workflow specialization.

workflow_stages.name: Service-support intake and prerequisite capture
workflow_stages.description: Validate mandatory tenant and program prerequisites before output generation; if missing, generate clarification and escalation output.
source_classes: Primary regulators, statutes, and official program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and artifact mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map requested outputs to required source artifacts across assessment records, incident systems, and reporting schedules.
source_classes: Primary regulators, statutes, and official policy source

workflow_stages.name: Output assembly and customer-stakeholder communication
workflow_stages.description: Build submission/disclosure outputs with explicit assumptions, open items, and tenant-specific routing notes.

workflow_stages.name: Conflict, exception, and escalation handling
workflow_stages.description: Stop confident recommendations when data conflicts or authority disagreements exist; produce escalation artifacts instead.
source_classes: Primary regulators, statutes, or official policy source
Primary regulators, statutes, and official program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Resolution confirmation and routing
workflow_stages.description: Record what is complete, what remains blocked, and what must be handed off before closure.

artifacts: Intake request record with program type, jurisdiction, and reporting cycle
Assessment records and reassessment outputs (e.g., MDS/OASIS-equivalent artifacts)
Incident management exports and response timelines
Service-plan or eligibility updates tied to person-centered outcomes
Submission package ledger with evidence traceability links
systems_of_record: CMS/Medicaid program reporting portals and data-quality interfaces
Tenant document repository and approval workflow

allowed: identify evidence dependencies and missing records
prepare disclosure-risk, escalation, and communication artifacts
escalate: missing jurisdictional program rules or submission deadlines
tenant privacy or legal authorization ambiguity
refuse: requests for regulator acceptance or guaranteed compliance
policy override requests not authorized in tenant scope

domain_failure_modes: Submission begins with insufficient jurisdictional, program, or period context.
Assessment and incident evidence is incomplete, stale, or contradictory.
Discrepancies across systems are concealed instead of surfaced and handed off.
Disclosure communication attempts bypass minimum-necessary and tenant authorization constraints.
State-level reporting requirements materially diverge from federal baseline and are treated as equivalent without explicit handoff.
Boundary drifts into clinical or legal interpretation work.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from industry name
Request requiring a more specialized adjacent lane
Requested claim not supported by evidence

unresolved_ambiguities: Specific local state portals and waiver-by-waiver metric calendars are tenant-specific; the package requires tenant-supplied local instructions before final completion.
resolved_ambiguities: No blocker-level ambiguity remains in this package because missing local details are modeled as prerequisite blockers with escalation requirements.
notes: The lane is materially distinct from a generic support specialist by enforcing elder-care and disability reporting authorities, disclosure controls, and HCBS/SNF/home-health evidence structure.
All escalations and refusals are evidence-driven and preserve adjacent-specialist boundaries.
